title: Where the consult flow lives
question: Should the feature-consult flow be a new subcommand in the decision-tree skill, a separate skill, or a plugin?
rationale: Make consult a 'plan' subcommand inside the existing skill, with its workflow in a bundled reference file — one schema, one set of wording rules, no drift. Chosen over a separate skill (duplicates the data model and competes for the trigger) and a plugin (premature).

options.label: One skill, 'plan' subcommand + bundled reference file

tradeoffs.note: one schema and style guide; no drift between copies
one clear description/trigger, no competing skills over the same data
progressive disclosure: the consult file loads only when planning
tied to the decision-tree skill, not independently invokable
the consult skill can be invoked on its own
the second skill must restate the data model — drift risk
two overlapping descriptions over the same data compete to trigger
a clean packaged unit to share
packaging overhead with no payoff until we actually distribute
